---
title: "Phase 4: QTL Detection & Assessment"
subtitle: "Detecting QTL intervals and evaluating simulation performance"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

The final phase of the pipeline takes GWA results from Phase 3, detects QTL
intervals, and assesses detection performance against the simulated causal
variants. Two parallel paths produce equivalent output:

- **DB path** (default) &mdash; writes results to a Parquet database and queries
  via DuckDB
- **Legacy path** (opt-in via `--legacy_assess`) &mdash; operates directly on
  intermediate files using R and GenomicRanges

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '17px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef db fill:#1a9e8f,stroke:#0e6b61,color:#fff
    classDef legacy fill:#d4a017,stroke:#9a7510,color:#fff
    classDef io fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    GWA(["GWA results: .fastGWA / .mlma (Phase 3)"]):::io

    subgraph DBPATH["DB Path (default)"]
        direction TB
        W1["WRITE_MARKER_SET (barrier)"]:::db
        W2["WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX"]:::db
        W3["WRITE_GWA_TO_DB"]:::db
        W4["WRITE_TRAIT_DATA"]:::db
        W5["AGGREGATE_METADATA (barrier)"]:::db
        AQ["ANALYZE_QTL (BF / EIGEN threshold)"]:::db
        AS["ASSESS_SIMS (var.exp ANOVA)"]:::db
        DBOUT(["db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv"]):::io
        W1 --> W3 --> W5
        W2 --> W5
        W4 --> W5
        W5 --> AQ --> AS --> DBOUT
    end

    subgraph LEGPATH["Legacy Path (--legacy_assess)"]
        direction TB
        INT["R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS"]:::legacy
        RSA["R_ASSESS_SIMS"]:::legacy
        LEGOUT(["simulation_assessment_results.tsv"]):::io
        INT --> RSA --> LEGOUT
    end

    GWA --> DBPATH
    GWA --> LEGPATH
```

Both paths expand each upstream GWA result by threshold &mdash; every
(mode, type) combination is analyzed once for BF and once for EIGEN &mdash;
doubling the output relative to Phase 3.

For a comparison of the two paths, see the
[Assessment Cross-Validation](cross-validation.qmd) documentation.


## Shared Concepts

Both paths implement the same logical algorithm for QTL interval detection.
The key concepts are described here once and referenced in the path-specific
sections below.


### Significance Thresholds

A significance threshold converts raw p-values into a binary significant/not
significant call for each marker. The pipeline supports three methods:

| Method | Formula | Source |
|--------|---------|--------|
| BF (Bonferroni) | `-log10(alpha / n_markers)` | Total marker count from the marker set |
| EIGEN | `-log10(alpha / n_independent_tests)` | Full eigenvalue decomposition of the genotype correlation matrix per chromosome, summing integer parts (λ > 1 → 1) and fractional parts of all eigenvalues (Li & Ji 2005) |
| User-defined | Numeric value used directly | Passed via `--sthresh` parameter |

Where `alpha` defaults to 0.05. The threshold is applied on the `-log10(p)`
scale.

**Source:** `R/analysis.R::calculate_threshold()`


### QTL Interval Detection Algorithm

Both paths apply the same four-step procedure to flag significant markers,
group them into QTL intervals, and define confidence intervals:

1. **Flag significant markers** &mdash; compute `-log10(p)` for each marker and
   flag those where `log10p >= threshold` as significant (`significant = 1`
   in DB path, `aboveBF = 1` in legacy path)

2. **Proportion check** &mdash; if more than 15% of markers are significant, the
   threshold is considered too lenient and QTL intervals are not defined (all
   interval columns set to `NA`)

3. **Group significant markers by proximity** &mdash; significant markers are
   assigned sequential `peak_id` values. Two significant markers on the same
   chromosome within `group_qtl` marker-index distance are assigned to the same
   peak; otherwise a new peak ID is assigned

4. **Define confidence intervals** &mdash; for each peak group, the CI boundary
   is set to `ci_size` markers beyond the outermost significant marker in the
   group, clamped to chromosome bounds. The peak marker is the one with the
   highest `-log10(p)` in the group

**Parameters:**

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `--group_qtl` | 1000 | Marker-index distance for grouping significant markers into the same QTL |
| `--ci_size` | 150 | Number of markers flanking the peak group to define the CI boundary |
| `--alpha` | 0.05 | Significance level for threshold calculation |

**Source (DB path):** `R/analysis.R::analyze_mapping()` which calls
`flag_significant_markers()` &rarr; `check_significant_proportion()` &rarr;
`group_markers_to_intervals()` &rarr; `define_confidence_intervals()`

**Source (legacy path):** `bin/Get_GCTA_Intervals.R::process_mapping_df()` which
implements the same logic inline


### Assessment Classification

After QTL intervals are defined, each QTL (simulated or detected) is classified
into one of four outcome categories:

| Designation | Simulated | Detected | Significant | Meaning |
|-------------|-----------|----------|-------------|---------|
| `Detected.CV` | TRUE | TRUE | TRUE | True positive &mdash; causal variant within a detected interval, peak is significant |
| `Missed.CV` | TRUE | FALSE | FALSE | False negative &mdash; causal variant not within any detected interval |
| `CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval` | TRUE | TRUE | FALSE | Causal variant within a detected interval, but its own marker is not significant |
| `False.Discovery` | FALSE | TRUE | TRUE | False positive &mdash; detected interval contains no causal variant |

**Source:** `R/sim_performance.R::designate_qtl()`


## Stage 1: Database Ingestion (DB Path Only)

The DB path writes marker sets and raw GWA results to a Hive-partitioned
Parquet database before performing QTL analysis. This enables DuckDB-based
queries at analysis time and decouples threshold application from data storage.

Five processes run during Stage 1. `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET` and
`DB_MIGRATION_AGGREGATE_METADATA` act as barriers. `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX`
and `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA` run in parallel as independent fire-and-forget
storage operations — they have no downstream dependents within the pipeline:


### 1. DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET

**Source:** `modules/db_migration/write_marker_set/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `resources/usr/bin/write_marker_set.R`

Writes the LD-pruned marker set and EIGEN independent tests count to the
Parquet database. One invocation per marker set (unique combination of group,
MAF, species, VCF release, and LD threshold).

**Command:**

```bash
write_marker_set.R --group ${group} --maf ${maf} --bim ${bim_file} \
    --eigen ${n_indep_tests} \
    --species ${species} --vcf_release_id ${vcf_release_id} --ms_ld ${ms_ld} \
    --base_dir ${base_dir}
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--group` | Population/strain group identifier |
| `--maf` | MAF threshold used for this marker set |
| `--bim` | Path to PLINK `.bim` file from `PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF` |
| `--eigen` | Path to independent tests file from `LOCAL_COMPILE_EIGENS` |
| `--species` | Species identifier (`c_elegans`, `c_briggsae`, etc.) |
| `--vcf_release_id` | 8-digit CaeNDR release date extracted from strainfile `vcf` column |
| `--ms_ld` | LD R² threshold used for marker SNP selection |
| `--base_dir` | Database output directory |

**R functions called:**

| Function | Source | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `generate_marker_set_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute deterministic 20-char SHA-256 marker set ID (v=2) from population, MAF, species, VCF release ID, and LD threshold |
| `read_bim_file()` | `R/io.R` | Read PLINK `.bim` file into a marker dataframe |
| `read_eigen_file()` | `R/io.R` | Read single-value EIGEN independent tests file |
| `write_marker_set()` | `R/database.R` | Write marker Parquet file and metadata |

**Barrier role:** All marker sets must complete before `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GWA_TO_DB`
proceeds. The pipeline collects all `done` signals and validates that at least one
marker set was written.

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `TO_SIMS.bim` | PLINK binary marker information file |
| Input | `*_total_independent_tests.txt` | EIGEN independent tests count |
| Output | `{base_dir}/markers/marker_sets/{marker_set_id}_markers.parquet` | Marker set Parquet file |
| Output | `{base_dir}/marker_set_metadata.parquet` | Marker set metadata |


### 2. DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX

**Source:** `modules/db_migration/write_genotype_matrix/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `resources/usr/bin/write_genotype_matrix.R`

Reads the wide-format genotype matrix TSV produced by `BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX`
(Phase 1), pivots it to long format, and writes a Parquet file per (group, maf)
combination. Runs in parallel with `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GWA_TO_DB` — no barrier
dependency in either direction.

**Command:**

```bash
write_genotype_matrix.R --group ${group} --maf ${maf} \
    --genotype_matrix ${genotype_matrix} --base_dir ${base_dir}
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--group` | Population/strain group identifier |
| `--maf` | MAF threshold used for this marker set |
| `--genotype_matrix` | Path to wide-format TSV from `BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX` |
| `--base_dir` | Database output directory |

**R functions called:**

| Function | Source | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `write_genotype_matrix()` | `R/database.R` | Pivot wide TSV to long format and write Parquet |

**Note:** `write_genotype_matrix()` calls `generate_marker_set_id()` internally to
construct the hash-based output path — it is not called directly in the script.

**Cardinality:** 1 invocation per (group, maf) — typically 2–4 tasks.

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `${group}_${maf}_Genotype_Matrix.tsv` | Wide-format genotype matrix from Phase 1 |
| Output | `{base_dir}/markers/genotypes/{marker_set_id}_genotypes.parquet` | Long-format genotype Parquet (CHROM, POS, strain, allele) |


### 3. DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GWA_TO_DB

**Source:** `modules/db_migration/write_gwa_to_db/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `resources/usr/bin/write_gwa_to_db.R`

Writes raw GWA output to Hive-partitioned Parquet. Runs once per (mode, type)
combination, **not** per threshold &mdash; thresholds are applied at query time
during `DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL`.

**Command:**

```bash
write_gwa_to_db.R \
    --group ${group} --maf ${maf} --nqtl ${nqtl} --effect ${effect} \
    --rep ${rep} --h2 ${h2} --mode ${mode} --type ${type} \
    --gwa_file ${gwa_file} --base_dir ${base_dir}
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--group` | Population/strain group identifier |
| `--maf` | MAF threshold |
| `--nqtl` | Number of simulated QTLs |
| `--effect` | Effect size distribution (e.g. `gamma`, `0.2-0.3`) |
| `--rep` | Simulation replicate number |
| `--h2` | Heritability |
| `--mode` | GWA mode (`inbred` or `loco`) |
| `--type` | PCA type (`pca` or `nopca`) |
| `--gwa_file` | Path to GCTA output file (`.fastGWA` or `.mlma`) |
| `--base_dir` | Database output directory |

**R functions called:**

| Function | Source | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `read_raw_gwa_file()` | `R/io.R` | Auto-detect and read `.fastGWA` or `.mlma` format |
| `generate_marker_set_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute parent marker-set hash from population and MAF |
| `generate_trait_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute trait hash from marker-set hash and simulation parameters |
| `generate_mapping_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute mapping hash from trait hash, algorithm, and PCA flag |
| `write_mapping_partitioned()` | `R/database.R` | Write to Hive-partitioned Parquet structure |

**LOCO deduplication:** LOCO `.mlma` files may contain duplicate marker rows.
The script applies `distinct(marker, .keep_all = TRUE)` before writing.

**Algorithm mapping:** The mode/type Nextflow values are translated to database
fields:

- `inbred` &rarr; `LMM-EXACT-INBRED`, `loco` &rarr; `LMM-EXACT-LOCO`
- `pca` &rarr; `pca = TRUE`, `nopca` &rarr; `pca = FALSE`

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `*.fastGWA` or `*.mlma` | Raw GCTA GWA output from `GCTA_PERFORM_GWA` |
| Output | `{base_dir}/mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/data.parquet` | Hive-partitioned mapping data |


### 4. DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA

**Source:** `modules/db_migration/write_trait_data/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `resources/usr/bin/write_trait_data.R`

Writes per-trait metadata, causal variants, and phenotype data to the database.
Sources the pre-upscaled `.phen` and `.par` files from `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES`
(Phase 2) via a `.multiMap` channel — before the mode-crossing at `GCTA_MAKE_GRM`.
Each trait is written exactly once regardless of how many mode/type combinations
are mapped.

**Command:**

```bash
write_trait_data.R --group ${group} --maf ${maf} --nqtl ${nqtl} \
    --effect ${effect} --rep ${rep} --h2 ${h2} \
    --pheno_file ${pheno_file} --par_file ${par_file} --base_dir ${base_dir}
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--group` | Population/strain group identifier |
| `--maf` | MAF threshold |
| `--nqtl` | Number of simulated QTLs |
| `--effect` | Effect size distribution |
| `--rep` | Simulation replicate number |
| `--h2` | Heritability |
| `--pheno_file` | Pre-upscaled `.phen` file from `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` |
| `--par_file` | Causal variant `.par` file from `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` |
| `--base_dir` | Database output directory |

**R functions called:**

| Function | Source | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `generate_marker_set_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute parent marker-set hash from population and MAF |
| `generate_trait_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute deterministic 20-char SHA-256 trait ID from marker-set hash and simulation parameters |
| `write_trait_metadata()` | `R/database.R` | Write per-trait parameter metadata |
| `write_causal_variants()` | `R/database.R` | Write causal variant positions and effects |
| `write_phenotype_data()` | `R/database.R` | Write per-strain phenotype values |

**Trait ID:** Computed via a two-step chain: `generate_marker_set_id(group, maf, species, vcf_release_id, ms_ld)` (v=2) yields the parent 20-char SHA-256 marker-set hash; `generate_trait_id(marker_set_hash, nqtl, effect, rep, h2)` yields the trait hash. All IDs are 20-character truncated SHA-256 hashes. No Groovy counterpart. Stable across `-resume`.

**Cardinality:** 1 invocation per unique trait (nqtl × rep × h2 × maf × effect × group).

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `*_sims.phen` | Pre-upscaled phenotype file from `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` |
| Input | `*_sims.par` | Causal variant parameters from `GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` |
| Output | `{base_dir}/traits/{trait_id}.parquet` | Per-trait metadata (parameters + trait_id) |
| Output | `{base_dir}/traits/causal_variants/{trait_id}_causal.parquet` | QTL positions, effects, frequencies |
| Output | `{base_dir}/traits/phenotypes/{trait_id}_phenotype.parquet` | Per-strain phenotype values |

::: {.callout-note}
The stored phenotype is pre-upscaled (GCTA output before `PYTHON_CHECK_VP`). Values
may differ from those used in the actual GWAS mapping by up to 1000× if `check_vp.py`
applied upscaling. This is intentional — the ANOVA SS ratio for offline
variance-explained estimation is scale-invariant.
:::


### 5. DB_MIGRATION_AGGREGATE_METADATA

**Source:** `modules/db_migration/aggregate_metadata/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `resources/usr/bin/aggregate_metadata.R`

Scans all mapping Parquet files and builds a metadata summary table. Runs once
after **all** `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GWA_TO_DB` processes complete.

**Command:**

```bash
aggregate_metadata.R ${base_dir}
```

The script reads the first row of each Parquet file to extract FK hash columns
(`mapping_id`, `marker_set_id`, `trait_id`) and counts total markers per file.

**Barrier role:** The database must be fully populated before analysis queries
begin. The `summary` output gates all downstream `DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL`
invocations.

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `{base_dir}/mappings/**/data.parquet` | All mapping Parquet files |
| Output | `{base_dir}/mappings_metadata.parquet` | Aggregated metadata table |
| Output | `aggregation_summary.txt` | Human-readable summary (barrier signal) |


### Database Structure

The Parquet database uses a Hive partitioning scheme:

```
{outputDir}/db/
 ├── markers/
 │   ├── marker_sets/
 │   │   └── {marker_set_id}_markers.parquet
 │   └── genotypes/
 │       └── {marker_set_id}_genotypes.parquet
 ├── mappings/
 │   └── population={pop}/
 │       └── mapping_id={id}/
 │           └── data.parquet
 ├── traits/
 │   ├── {trait_id}.parquet                      ◄── trait metadata
 │   ├── causal_variants/
 │   │   └── {trait_id}_causal.parquet           ◄── causal variant parameters
 │   └── phenotypes/
 │       └── {trait_id}_phenotype.parquet        ◄── pre-upscaled phenotype values
 ├── mappings_metadata.parquet
 └── marker_set_metadata.parquet
```

At query time, DuckDB reads the partitioned Parquet files directly via the
`read_parquet()` function with `hive_partitioning = true`. This allows
efficient filtering by population and mapping ID without loading all data into
memory. See `R/queries.R::open_mapping_db()` for the connection setup.

The `traits/` directory and its `causal_variants/` and `phenotypes/` subdirectories
are written by `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA` and are not read by any pipeline
process — they are stored for future offline analysis. See
[Database Structure](database-structure.qmd#trait-data) for full schemas.


## Stage 2: QTL Detection


### DB Path: DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL

**Source:** `modules/db_migration/analyze_qtl/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `resources/usr/bin/analyze_qtl.R`

Queries the populated database, applies the significance threshold, detects QTL
intervals, and writes a QTL regions TSV. Runs once per upstream parameter
combination &times; threshold (BF and EIGEN), doubling the cardinality relative
to Phase 3.

**Command:**

```bash
analyze_qtl.R \
    --group ${group} --maf ${maf} --nqtl ${nqtl} --effect ${effect} \
    --rep ${rep} --h2 ${h2} --mode ${mode} --type ${type} \
    --threshold ${threshold} \
    --base_dir ${base_dir} --ci_size ${ci_size} --snp_grouping ${snp_grouping} \
    --alpha ${alpha}
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--threshold` | Threshold method: `BF` or `EIGEN` |
| `--base_dir` | Database root directory |
| `--ci_size` | Number of flanking markers for CI boundary |
| `--snp_grouping` | Marker-index distance for grouping significant markers |
| `--alpha` | Significance level |

**R functions called:**

| Function | Source | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `generate_marker_set_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute parent marker-set hash |
| `generate_trait_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute trait hash from marker-set hash and simulation params |
| `generate_mapping_id()` | `R/database.R` | Compute mapping hash for database lookup |
| `query_for_threshold_analysis()` | `R/queries.R` | Query mapping data from Parquet DB via DuckDB |
| `get_threshold_params()` | `R/database.R` | Retrieve marker count and EIGEN independent tests |
| `calculate_threshold()` | `R/analysis.R` | Compute `-log10(p)` threshold value |
| `analyze_mapping()` | `R/analysis.R` | Full QTL detection pipeline (flag &rarr; check &rarr; group &rarr; CI) |
| `extract_qtl_regions()` | `R/analysis.R` | Summarize one row per QTL interval |

**Note:** The `.par` file (simulated causal variants) is staged as input but
**not used** by this process &mdash; it is passed through to `DB_MIGRATION_ASSESS_SIMS`.

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | Parquet database | GWA results and marker sets (queried via DuckDB) |
| Input | `*.par` | Causal variant file (pass-through only) |
| Output | `*_qtl_regions.tsv` | QTL regions with peak_id, startPOS, peakPOS, endPOS, interval_size |


### Legacy Path: R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS

**Source:** `modules/r/get_gcta_intervals/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `bin/Get_GCTA_Intervals.R`

Reads the raw GWA output file directly (not from a database), applies the
significance threshold, detects QTL intervals, and calculates variance explained
(r&sup2;) for each significant marker.

**Command:**

```bash
Rscript --vanilla Get_GCTA_Intervals.R \
    ${genotype_matrix} ${pheno_file} ${gwa_file} \
    ${n_indep_tests} ${nqtl} ${rep} ${qtl_group_size} ${qtl_ci_size} \
    ${h2} ${threshold} ${group} ${maf} ${effect} ${mode}_${type} ${alpha}
```

| Argument | Position | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| Genotype matrix | 1 | TSV genotype matrix from `BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX` |
| Phenotype file | 2 | GCTA `.pheno` file |
| GWA file | 3 | GCTA output (`.fastGWA` or `.mlma`) |
| Independent tests | 4 | EIGEN independent tests count |
| nQTL | 5 | Number of simulated QTLs |
| Replicate | 6 | Simulation replicate number |
| SNP grouping | 7 | Marker-index distance for grouping (`--group_qtl`) |
| CI size | 8 | Flanking markers for CI boundary (`--ci_size`) |
| h2 | 9 | Simulated heritability |
| Threshold | 10 | Threshold method (`BF`, `EIGEN`, or numeric) |
| Group | 11 | Strain set identifier |
| MAF | 12 | MAF threshold |
| Effect | 13 | Effect size distribution |
| Mode | 14 | `{mode}_{type}` string (e.g. `inbred_pca`) |
| Alpha | 15 | Significance level (default 0.05) |

The core function `process_mapping_df()` implements the same four-step algorithm
described in [Shared Concepts](#qtl-interval-detection-algorithm) above,
plus an additional variance explained calculation using Pearson r&sup2; between
phenotype values and allelic state at significant markers.

::: {.callout-note}
## Key Differences from DB Path

- Reads raw GWA file directly (no database dependency)
- Requires genotype matrix for variance explained (r&sup2;) calculation
- Adds `var.exp` column to processed mapping output
- No DuckDB dependency
:::

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `*_Genotype_Matrix.tsv` | Numeric genotype matrix |
| Input | `*_sims.pheno` | GCTA phenotype file |
| Input | `*.fastGWA` or `*.mlma` | Raw GCTA GWA output |
| Input | `*_total_independent_tests.txt` | EIGEN independent tests count |
| Output | `*_processed_LMM-EXACT_{mode}_{type}_mapping.tsv` | Processed mapping with QTL annotations |
| Output | `*_LMM-EXACT_{mode}_{type}_qtl_region.tsv` | QTL region summary |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Algorithmic Equivalence

Both paths implement the same QTL detection algorithm. The
[Assessment Cross-Validation](cross-validation.qmd) framework confirms
identical results between the two paths.
:::


## Stage 3: Performance Assessment


### DB Path: DB_MIGRATION_ASSESS_SIMS

**Source:** `modules/db_migration/assess_sims/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `resources/usr/bin/assess_sims.R`

Reads the QTL regions from `DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL`, loads causal variants
from the `.par` file, re-queries the mapping data for peak annotations, and
compiles the full assessment by matching simulated and detected QTLs.

**Command:**

```bash
assess_sims.R \
    --group ${group} --maf ${maf} --nqtl ${nqtl} --effect ${effect} \
    --rep ${rep} --h2 ${h2} --mode ${mode} --type ${type} \
    --threshold ${threshold} --par_file ${par_file} \
    --qtl_regions ${qtl_regions} \
    --base_dir ${base_dir} --ci_size ${ci_size} --snp_grouping ${snp_grouping} \
    --alpha ${alpha}
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--par_file` | Path to `.par` file with simulated causal variants |
| `--qtl_regions` | Path to QTL regions TSV from `analyze_qtl.R` |

**R functions called:**

| Function | Source | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `load_causal_variants()` | `R/assessment.R` | Read `.par` file, parse `CHROM:POS` format |
| `score_causal_markers()` | `R/assessment.R` | Match causal variants to mapping data for log10p and significance |
| `find_peak_causal_overlaps()` | `R/assessment.R` | Position-based overlap: CHROM match + POS within [startPOS, endPOS] |
| `build_assessment_union()` | `R/assessment.R` | Combine simulated + detected into unified assessment dataframe |
| `compile_full_assessment()` | `R/assessment.R` | Orchestrate the above helpers |
| `format_assessment_tsv()` | `R/assessment.R` | Select and order columns to match standardized output schema |

**Overlap detection:** Uses position-based matching &mdash; a causal variant is
considered overlapping a detected QTL if its chromosome matches and its position
falls within the interval `[startPOS, endPOS]`.

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `*_qtl_regions.tsv` | QTL regions from `DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL` |
| Input | `*.par` | Simulated causal variant file |
| Input | Parquet database | Re-queried for peak marker annotations |
| Output | `*_db_assessment.tsv` | Per-combination assessment results |

All per-combination outputs are collected into the final
`db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv`.


### Legacy Path: R_ASSESS_SIMS

**Source:** `modules/r/assess_sims/main.nf`
&nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; **Script:** `bin/Assess_Sims.R`

Loads the processed mapping output from `R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS`, the genotype
matrix, and the `.par` file to compile the assessment.

**Command:**

```bash
Rscript --vanilla Assess_Sims.R \
    ${processed_mapping} ${genotype_matrix} ${par_file} ${pheno_file} \
    ${nqtl} ${rep} ${h2} ${maf} ${effect} ${group} ${mode}_${type}_${threshold} \
    ${alpha} ${ci_size} ${snp_grouping}
```

| Argument | Position | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| Processed mapping | 1 | Processed mapping TSV from `R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS` |
| Genotype matrix | 2 | Numeric genotype matrix |
| Par file | 3 | Simulated causal variant `.par` file |
| Phenotype file | 4 | GCTA `.pheno` file |
| nQTL | 5 | Number of simulated QTLs |
| Replicate | 6 | Simulation replicate number |
| h2 | 7 | Simulated heritability |
| MAF | 8 | MAF threshold |
| Effect | 9 | Effect size distribution |
| Group | 10 | Strain set identifier |
| Algorithm ID | 11 | `{mode}_{type}_{threshold}` (e.g. `inbred_pca_BF`) |
| Alpha | 12 | Significance level (default 0.05) |
| CI size | 13 | CI boundary in markers (default 150) |
| SNP grouping | 14 | Marker-index grouping distance (default 1000) |

::: {.callout-note}
## Key Differences from DB Path

- Uses `GenomicRanges::findOverlapPairs()` for overlap detection (vs.
  position-based matching in the DB path)
- Calculates simulated variance explained via ANOVA (`aov(trait.value ~ allele)`)
  &mdash; the DB path sets `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` to `NA`
- Requires the genotype matrix as input
- No DuckDB dependency
:::

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `*_processed_LMM-EXACT_{mode}_{type}_mapping.tsv` | Processed mapping from `R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS` |
| Input | `*_Genotype_Matrix.tsv` | Numeric genotype matrix |
| Input | `*.par` | Simulated causal variant file |
| Input | `*.pheno` | GCTA phenotype file |
| Output | `*_{mode}_{type}_{threshold}_mapping.tsv` | Per-combination assessment results |

All per-combination outputs are collected into the final
`simulation_assessment_results.tsv`.


### Assessment Output Schema

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Full Column Reference (35 columns)

The assessment output files (`db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv` and
`simulation_assessment_results.tsv`) share a standardized column schema.

**QTL identity:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `QTL` | Peak marker ID for the QTL interval (`CHROM:POS` format) |
| `CHROM` | Chromosome (numeric ID: 1 = I, 2 = II, etc.) |
| `POS` | Position of the marker |

**Classification:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `Simulated` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` &mdash; whether the QTL was among the simulated causal variants |
| `Detected` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` &mdash; whether the QTL was detected in a mapping interval |

**Simulated variant info:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `RefAllele` | Reference allele for the causal marker |
| `Frequency` | Allele frequency of the causal marker |
| `Effect` | Simulated effect size |
| `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` | Variance explained by the simulated QTL (ANOVA in legacy path; `NA` in DB path) |

**Mapping statistics:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `log10p` | `-log10(p-value)` of the causal marker from mapping |
| `significant` | `1`/`0` &mdash; whether the causal marker exceeds the significance threshold |
| `BETA` | Effect size estimate from mapping for the peak marker |

**Interval info:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `startPOS` | Start position of the QTL confidence interval |
| `peakPOS` | Position of the peak marker in the interval |
| `endPOS` | End position of the QTL confidence interval |
| `detected.peak` | Marker ID of the detected peak in the interval |
| `interval.log10p` | `-log10(p-value)` of the peak marker in the interval |
| `interval.var.exp` | Variance explained by the peak marker (r&sup2; in legacy path; `NA` in DB path) |
| `interval.Frequency` | Allele frequency of the peak marker |
| `peak_id` | Numeric ID of the QTL interval (1, 2, ..., n) |
| `interval_size` | Size of the QTL interval in base pairs |
| `top.hit` | `TRUE`/`FALSE` &mdash; whether the QTL marker is the top hit in the interval |

**Simulation parameters:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `nQTL` | Number of QTLs simulated for the trait |
| `simREP` | Simulation replicate number |
| `h2` | Simulated heritability |
| `maf` | MAF threshold used in simulation |
| `effect_distribution` | Effect size range used in simulation |
| `strain_set_id` | Name of the strain set |

**Mapping method:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `mode` | GWA mode (`inbred` or `loco`) |
| `type` | PCA type (`pca` or `nopca`) |
| `threshold` | Threshold method (`BF` or `EIGEN`) |
| `algorithm_id` | Full mapping method identifier |

**Analysis parameters:**

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `alpha` | Significance level used for threshold calculation |
| `ci_size` | Number of flanking markers used for CI definition |
| `snp_grouping` | Marker-index distance used for grouping |

**Note on `algorithm_id` format:** The DB path uses uppercase identifiers
(e.g. `LMM-EXACT-INBRED_PCA_EIGEN`) while the legacy path uses lowercase
(e.g. `inbred_pca_EIGEN`). The
[Assessment Cross-Validation](cross-validation.qmd) documentation explains the
normalization applied for comparison.
:::
